timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: Stage 4 construction commences
timeline.detail: Wartsila announced fourth stage expansion (360MWh addition), completion expected early 2027.

timeline.title: Stage 1 enters commercial operations (460MW/1,770MWh)
timeline.detail: Origin commenced commercial operations of Eraring Battery 1 at the site of the 2,880MW Eraring coal plant.

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2025-12 to 2025-12-18 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

eis_specs.document_title: Eraring Big Battery — State Significant Development Assessment (NSW IPC SSD-2022-0119)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Eraring 330 kV substation (TransGrid), Lake Macquarie NSW (on-site, former power station)
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: On-site — located within the Eraring Power Station compound on Lake Macquarie, NSW. Former 2,880 MW coal plant 330 kV substation reused.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: IMPORTANT NOTE: This is a separate battery from the existing eraring-battery (150 MW Tesla Megapack). The Eraring Big Battery (700 MW Wartsila Quantum3) is a different, larger project also at the Eraring site. Both batteries use the same TransGrid 330 kV Eraring substation, eliminating major connection capex.
eis_specs.notes: 700 MW / 1,390 MWh (2-hour) BESS at Eraring Power Station, Lake Macquarie NSW. Developer: Origin Energy. Technology: Wartsila Quantum3 (LFP, CATL). EPC: Enerven. NSW IPC SSD-2022-0119. Now operating. Distinct from the 150 MW eraring-battery (Tesla Megapack). Both batteries exploit the exceptional TransGrid 330 kV infrastructure at this former 2,880 MW coal plant.
